title: digest test : make more robust
body:
Instead of relying on random images from various separate
repositories, some of which are actually not the schema
version we thought we were testing (and some of which are
on unreliable registries, which causes test flakes),
craft our own images and store them all on quay.io

This commit:

  1) Adds a new script (tests/digest/make-v2sN) that
     crafts a custom image with the desired attributes:
     schema version 1 or 2, possibly with a duplicate
     layer. Script decides based on its name, that is,
     we are expected to have symlinks pointing to it.

  2) Updates digest.bats to use these newly-made images.

  3) Extends digest.bats by adding a check for 'v2s*'
     in the image name; if found, we now use 'imgtype'
     to extract the manifest and confirm that the
     pulled image has the expected schema version (1 or 2).

  4) Extends digest.bats by adding a check for duplicate
     layers in the one test that requires them.

Pushing the generated images to quay.io is left as an
exercise for the reader; as is updating the Digest SHAs
and/or tags in the individual tests in digest.bats

Fixes: #2147

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I dare say you've outdone yourself this time @edsantiago 
It LGTM, but I'd like a head nod from @nalind about the digest testing as that's his baileywick.
@nalind could I ask for your opinion and perhaps blessing on this?
@nalind PTAL so we can get this merged.

Buildah gating tests are [failing on f32](https://jenkins-continuous-infra.apps.ci.centos.org/blue/organizations/jenkins/fedora-f32-build-pipeline/detail/fedora-f32-build-pipeline/63/pipeline/#step-248-log-1061), in digest tests. I saw similar failures on (manual) f31 tests yesterday.

Could we pretty-please get this reviewed and merged so we can all waste less time reviewing test failures?

Well, I've given up. I can't figure out a way to create an equivalent container and preserve the characteristics when pushed to `quay.io`; I'm assuming that the bad whalesay image is a product of an earlier time, and all tools have checks now to make it impossible to push such an image to a modern repo.

I've removed the now-unused 'make-dup' functionality, but am leaving the `make-v2sN` script because it's still used for two other images and I believe it's important to have reproducible test images. I've undone the `quay.io/whalesay` edit, reverting back to `docker.io/whalesay`, and have added a minimal sanity check to make sure we don't accidentally go back to a different image. I've also added two retries in hopes of alleviating the common whalesay flakes.

I'd still like to get this reviewed and merged, because I still believe it's a more accurate and robust test.

Gating tests [are failing](https://jenkins-continuous-infra.apps.ci.centos.org/blue/organizations/jenkins/fedora-rawhide-build-pipeline/detail/fedora-rawhide-build-pipeline/9993/pipeline/#step-248-log-1060); this PR would fix that.
LGTM and happy green test buttons.  Would like a head nod from @nalind if possible before merging.

Can someone with privs rerun the failing test? I think it's a flake.

The more I look at it, the less I understand it: the test that failed is `something-http-pre` and is bytewise identical to the test immediately after, which is `something-http-post`... which passes.  There are two errors:
```
[+0312s] # 2020/03/25 20:14:00 listen tcp :33192: bind: address already in use
[+0312s] # error prepping temporary context directory: error getting "http://0.0.0.0:33192/context.tar": Get http://0.0.0.0:33192/context.tar: dial tcp 0.0.0.0:33192: connect: connection refused
```
I'm guessing the first one is from `httpd`, the second from `buildah` (the helper script starts httpd but doesn't actually confirm that it succeeds). I don't know why port 33192 would be in use.

Finally, I can't reproduce it on my own test setup. Has anyone seen this before?

Yay, tests green!

For ease of review, I've left this as two commits. The second is the one that adds and documents the dup-layer image.

Followup: buildah gating tests [are green](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2020-7f2d2a418f); yesterday's errors (timeouts on docker.io) probably deserve investigation and fixing so we continue removing our reliance on docker.io, but I can get to that next week.

Thanks again @nalind for your patience and help with this one.
